policy: Important NPCs must be generated from profiles, use MCA-medieval-style skins where possible, visibly carry/wear real indexed gear, and have a gameplay purpose. Jayden should not have to hand-build the standard set.

roles.behavior: social anchor; explains Guild, ranks, contracts, and Codex
roles.interaction_owner: CustomNPCs script now; Ascendant NPC Runtime later

roles.behavior: rank-trial authority; must never show as Unranked
roles.interaction_owner: CustomNPCs script now; FTB Quest/rank hooks later

roles.behavior: points players to Bountiful contracts and danger tiers
roles.interaction_owner: CustomNPCs script now; Bountiful/FTB hooks later

roles.behavior: settlement defense identity and local threat warnings
roles.interaction_owner: CustomNPCs or Guard Villagers bridge later

roles.behavior: explains Iron's Spells, scroll limits, magic loot, and Arcanist path
roles.interaction_owner: CustomNPCs script now; Iron's Spells hooks later

roles.behavior: world flavor and future competitive hunter encounters
roles.interaction_owner: CustomNPCs now; Ascendant NPC Runtime for combat logic later

roles.behavior: local settlement identity, rumors, and threat warnings
roles.interaction_owner: Easy NPC or MCA-friendly flow later

equipment_rules: Every visible weapon, shield, armor piece, hat, or relic must exist in config/ascendant_index/gear_registry.json.
NPC gear can be no-drop identity gear; rival and combat NPC drops must be deliberately authored.
Social NPCs should use readable silhouettes, not random max-tier gear.
Rival hunters can exceed local tier only with an explicit story/drop policy.
next_live_rules: Use generated_npc_profiles.json and spawn_set functions for standard NPC sets.
If CustomNPCs behavior cannot defend settlements or hold formation, build Ascendant NPC Runtime instead of manually scripting every NPC.
